description:
Spec §1 claims the per-clone JSON-lines log stays corruption-free under parallel-agent writes: "One object per line keeps concurrent appends from parallel agents atomic (sub-PIPE_BUF, O_APPEND)." That guarantee is conditional and currently unenforced:

- PIPE_BUF is 4096 on Linux, and the atomic-append-on-overflow guarantee is really a *pipe* property; for regular files, atomicity of a single large write() is not portably guaranteed. A record longer than PIPE_BUF can interleave with another agent's record and corrupt the log.
- The records most likely to overflow are exactly the ones the log exists to capture: §6 envelopes *plugin stderr* line-by-line ("balls pipes the child's stderr and ENVELOPES each line"), which is unbounded — a stack trace, a long git error, a diff line.

So the failure mode (parallel agents + a verbose/failing plugin) is the design's stated use case.

Fix options (pick on implementation, in bl-2e9f):
1. Truncate/escape each enveloped line to < PIPE_BUF before append (keeps the lock-free fast path; lossy on giant lines but logs shouldn't carry them).
2. Take a short advisory flock around the append (robust for any size; small contention cost).
A note in the skill/spec that log lines are bounded is warranted either way.

Relates to bl-2e9f (unified per-clone JSON-lines log) — this is a correctness requirement on that work, surfaced in the spec attack (post-freeze). If §1's atomicity sentence stays, it needs the bound; a §15 revision-log entry should record it.
title: Unified log: bound enveloped line length so concurrent appends stay atomic
